documents.title: Montana State University public AI posture sources

ai_strategy.summary: Montana State's public AI posture centers on faculty scholarship support, MSU Library/Research CyberInfrastructure collaboration, a graduate AI certificate, and AI/ML work in Montana NSF EPSCoR.

key_provisions: Use institutionally reviewed AI tools for sensitive university work.
Protect restricted, confidential, student, personnel, and research data when using AI tools.
Verify AI output and keep humans accountable for academic, administrative, and research decisions.
ai_policy.summary: Montana State University's public AI policy posture is cautious and emphasizes responsible use, privacy, and human accountability based on reviewed sources.

bodies.name: Montana NSF EPSCoR AIML team
bodies.scope: Montana State's public AI posture centers on faculty scholarship support, MSU Library/Research CyberInfrastructure collaboration, a graduate AI certificate, and AI/ML work in Montana NSF EPSCoR.

governance_structure.summary: Governance appears distributed across AI-facing offices, centers, technology units, teaching units, or committees; no standalone chief AI officer was confirmed unless named in leadership.

initiatives.title: Responsible AI guidance and governance
initiatives.description: Montana State University has public AI guidance, governance, or strategy resources that frame responsible adoption, privacy, academic integrity, and human oversight.
initiatives.significance: This is a concrete R1 signal of AI moving from isolated experimentation toward governed, mission-linked adoption.

investment.description: No public dollar amount found in reviewed sources.

initiatives.title: CatChat on-premise generative AI portal
initiatives.description: CatChat is a free generative AI portal for all MSU students, faculty, and staff, hosted on MSU systems and powered by the Tempest supercomputer.
initiatives.significance: This closes Montana State’s platform gap with a campus-wide, institution-hosted LLM service rather than only external tools.

investment.description: Access and security page says CatChat is free to faculty, staff, and students and supported by technology fees; no total cost disclosed.

collaborations.description: Institution-hosted service maintained by MSU Research Computing.

initiatives.policy_implications: Keeps data in-house and approves public/restricted data use under MSU data stewardship standards.

initiatives.title: Provost AI resources and Copilot options
initiatives.description: MSU Provost AI resources identify CatChat, Microsoft Copilot Chat, and paid Microsoft 365 Copilot, including a public $216 per-user annual price for the enhanced Copilot tier.
initiatives.significance: This closes part of the platform/cost gap with specific tool and pricing evidence.

investment.funding_source: Per-user department or individual Microsoft 365 Copilot cost
investment.description: MSU lists Microsoft 365 Copilot as available at an additional cost of $216 per user per year.

collaborations.description: MSU lists Microsoft Copilot tools as part of the institutional AI resource menu.

tools_and_vendors.name: Microsoft Copilot Chat / Microsoft 365 Copilot
tools_and_vendors.role: AI assistant options in the MSU tool portfolio

initiatives.policy_implications: Encourages tool selection based on data sensitivity and workflow needs.

enterprise_platforms.vendor: Montana State University Research Computing Infrastructure

computing_infrastructure.summary: MSU CatChat is maintained by Research Computing with computational resources provided by the Tempest supercomputer, which the public pages describe as powering the on-premise generative AI service; a public total GPU count for Tempest was not found in this sweep.

regulatory_context.summary: State/system context was checked for MT. Relevant public AI or system governance links are included where found.

narrative_summary: Montana State University is an R1 institution whose public AI posture is best understood as follows: Montana State's public AI posture centers on faculty scholarship support, MSU Library/Research CyberInfrastructure collaboration, a graduate AI certificate, and AI/ML work in Montana NSF EPSCoR. The evidence touches governance and policy, teaching and learning, research and innovation, enterprise enablement, student experience, workforce development, and equity or public-impact dimensions. The strongest public sources are first-party AI hubs, guidance pages, research center pages, academic-program materials, and state or system policy resources. Compared with the most centralized AI adopters, several details remain opaque, including named executive AI ownership, exact enterprise user counts, and total AI spending. Even so, the reviewed public record supports a medium-confidence assessment that AI is moving into curriculum, research infrastructure, technology governance, and student or workforce-facing programs rather than remaining purely ad hoc.

gaps_acknowledged: No public chief AI officer was confirmed in reviewed sources.
No total institution-wide AI investment amount was found.
Exact enterprise AI user counts or contract values were usually not public.
MSU now discloses an on-premise CatChat platform and Microsoft 365 Copilot per-user cost, but public sources still do not disclose CatChat usage counts or Tempest GPU totals.
Worker 3 did not find public student subgroup outcome measures for CatChat or AI literacy resources.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: balanced enterprise.
